module_mood_contract: learning through contrast — 'I can feel what packaging adds now; I couldn't have read it as a lecture'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — module honours its deliberate mood.
Epistemic mood not punitive — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity — no front-running, sequencing matches strategy doc.
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts instances: 90%-correct ('No benchmark told you what went wrong; the artefact did'), self-aware/grain-of-salt ('Remember: the artefact rules, self-reports don't'), compounding ('Your verifier is your first eval').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — gate-then-decorate, breadcrumbs on survivors only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All six bullets lead with discipline. One breadcrumb ('**Remember: the artefact rules, self-reports don't.**') on the self-awareness theme bullet.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lectures/what-packaging-is.md placed at line 62, after exercise + Debrief/re-send. Opener lectures arm the student; closer names the pattern from felt evidence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two opener lectures follow the prescribed arm-the-student shape. Ronacher/Cherny names held for the closer per strategy doc § M5 detail.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both forcing functions are in registry markers ({{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} line 28, {{prompt:ae101-m5-rerun-packaged}} line 56). Body prose after re-send prompt (lines 58-59) is an optional recovery move within the carve-out for optional refinement moves.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversation cues.
Plug points + UX — pluggable and operable.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Plug points (lines 118-121) name concrete integration commitments. Fast-operator path: worktree-setup prompt → exercise (separate file) → re-send prompt is coherent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 123-127: three falsifiable leap tests present with named verbs and falsification conditions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artefact contracts table — every produced file has a contract row.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 128-138: full table with stable identifier, producing prompt, and consuming module(s) for all six artefacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 140-151: eight rows covering every phase forcing function with dominant failure and escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module rescue callout at module open when prior send-off can plausibly fail.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:36 '## Start here' body: 'You walk in with the artefact in front of you.' No student-facing rescue callout or accept-loss floor present. M5 load-bears on M4's overnight un-packaged run which by design underdelivers and can also be thin, stalled, or inaccessible. Maintainer block has Connections-blocker push-back move (line 96) but it is not visible to the student.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add two consecutive blockquote callouts at ## Start here: (1) rescue gate naming the M4 transcript recovery path and the fresh-open move; (2) accept-loss floor naming which exercise phases still deliver without a full M4 artifact. Both are carve-outs to check_student_facing.md rule 5 — add maintainer-block note documenting the exception.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts in registry, referenced by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} at line 28 and {{prompt:ae101-m5-rerun-packaged}} at line 56 — registry markers with lead-in sentences.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Git-output greps with multi-result fallback.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 29-30: body callout immediately after the worktree-setup marker names the multi-result case and provides fallback (filter by 'M4 starting point' log message; fall back to SHA in transcript). Option A shape per rule 33.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase scores: [null (setup/exempt), 8 (start-here+exercise), 8 (re-send), 9 (closing lecture)]. Close: 9. No teaching beat below 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — contrast stays live at M5 close.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Re-send is sent but not read within M5. Closing lecture names the three-pattern but the second run is still running. 'Bring to Module 6' defers the read explicitly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — contrast reliably triggers across persona variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment structurally enforced by two-run arc. For students with a usable M4 artifact (modal case), the contrast fires reliably. Missing rescue callout (rule 54) creates fragility for the thin-artifact edge case but does not prevent the teaching moment from landing in the modal case.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module leads with and honours the Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'The contrast is the lesson.' Module structure is entirely built around the two-run contrast: diagnose M4 failures → earn the three-pattern from them → re-send packaged. Honored in every section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to M6 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 64-66: 'Module 6 reads the packaged run. Subtler misses, subtler drift.' Matches M6 strategy doc open hook exactly.
